short_description: Onsite Resource Availability - Team Coverage Withdrawn (Days) - DAL14
long_description:
Affected Support: On-site personnel coverage
Location: Dal14
Ensuing shift coverage at Dal14 for the day shift from 12:00-18:00 UTC (0600-1200 CST). During this time, we will not be able to respond to on-site requests and/or troubleshooting. In the event of potential power or cooling events identified through monitoring, or SEV1 & SEV2 customer-related tickets and cases, please use the Data Center escalation points per https://confluence.softlayer.local/display/DCOps/DCO+Emergency+Contact+List

short_description: is04.sr01.sao04 switch down
close_notes: NRE has worked with datacenter staff to replace the faulty hardware with spare hardware. An RMA case has been initiated to replenish the failed hardware.
long_description:
Internal switch is04.sr01.sao04 has gone down.

As of 21:45 UTC 30 November 2025, Network Specialists are investigating an internal switch that services provision servers in SAO04 Datacenter. Customer may experience delays in provisions while Networking and Datacenter staff work on restoring the internal switch. 

As of 00:38 UTC 01 December 2025, Network Specialists worked with Datacenter staff on replacing the internal switch and recovered provision server access to SAO04. 

https://github.ibm.com/IaaS-NRE/nreincidents/issues/19665

No EMS - Services teams will provide communication to customers for any stalled txns.

short_description: Internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 is down. Provisioning in Classic may be impacted
close_notes:
Network teams have replaced the faulty switch with the local data-center team on shift. Following the hardware replacement, internal teams confirmed that all stalled (test) transactions have moved on following a restart.

---

Summary details 
(All Timestamps must be in UTC) 
Issue Summary: Internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 is down. BM & VSI provisioning in Classic may be impacted
SME Findings:  N/A
Parent INC: INC10443177
Reason of Escalation: N/A
Resolution Steps: IaaS Core (NRE) worked with DCOPS to replace the faulty switch. All stalled transactions (test) moved on after manual restart.
Signature of Issue: 
Error while preregistering with Redhat Satellite: Could not initialize Redhat api client: There was an error while communicating with the Redhat satellite. Cause: HttpCode 0 during call: 'https://rhnsatsao0401a.softlayer.local:443/katello/api/v2/organizations/customer' - curl error: Failed to connect to rhnsatsao0401a.softlayer.local port 443: Connection timed out
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: N/A
Category/Subcategory: Network Network hardware Network Switch
Timeline Summary: 
2025-11-30 21:45:00 IaaS Core (NRE) noticed an issue internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 and created INC10443177
2025-11-30 21:54:00 IaaS Core (NRE) notified #sre-to-sre about the network outage event impacting is04.sr01.sao04
2025-11-30 22:03:27 IaaS Core (NRE) notified #core-team-discussion about the network outage event impacting is04.sr01.sao04
2025-11-30 22:15:00 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) ran test RedHat VSI provision in SAO04
2025-11-30 22:15:42 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Redhat VSI test provision failed at Physical License Register.
2025-11-30 22:32:55 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) created Classic - Normal Sev4 INC10443479 
2025-11-30 22:34:35 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) opened #warroom-classic-inc10443479
2025-11-30 22:49:01 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) notified Compute Infrastructure for awareness regarding rhnsatsao0401 on #compinfra-sre-esc. The latter was already aware of the NRE incident.
2025-11-30 23:07:47 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Microsoft Windows Server 2019 VSI provision (with secondary software) completed without issue
2025-11-30 23:12:58 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Rocky Linux 9 VSI provision completed without issue
2025-11-30 23:15:00 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) confirmed that only RHEL provisioning transactions will be impacted
2025-11-30 23:18:00 IaaS Core (NRE) is working on preparing for the switch replacement with DCOPS. No customer impact observed for provisioning
2025-11-30 23:48:00 DC started the switch replacement.
2025-12-01 00:38:44 IaaS Core (NRE) confirmed the switch replacement has completed and the ports are enabled. All failed test RHEL transactions were restarted and confirmed moving by IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: N/A
Warroom: #warroom-classic-inc10443479
Meeting URL: N/A
Outage Start: 2025-11-30 21:45:00
Outage End: 2025-12-01 00:38:44 
Detection Time: 2025-11-30 22:15:42
Identification Time: 2025-11-30 22:15:42
Mitigation Time: 2025-12-01 00:38:44 
CIE pilot/co-pilot/squad names: Venkat
CORE Manager names: JOBY GEORGE 
Executives(VPs) names: N/A
Team mitigated the CIE: IaaS Core (NRE)
Next Update Time: N/A
long_description:
At 2025-11-30 21:45:00 UTC IBM Cloud engineers have been alerted by NRE team, to an issue with internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04. Customers attempting  to provision VSIs in SAO04 may experience longer than usual provisioning times or failed transactions. 

IBM Cloud engineers are currently investigating the issue  and will continue to provide updates until the issue is resolved.

short_description: Customers in BR-SAO may see 500s
long_description: Customers in the BR-SAO region may experience intermittent 500 errors from services hosted in SAO04 due to a network issue.
